news.title:

Bank facilities remain unchanged; details provided in annexure.

news.title:
Borosil Renewables faces higher fuel costs but expects to hold 30% margins.
Gas supply disruption forces market purchases; alternate backup in place for stability.

news.title: Borosil Renewables reports contracts impacted by force majeure but operations remain normal.

news.title:
Borosil Renewables plans expansion amid rising solar glass demand.
China's market risks highlighted by Pradeep Kheruka during discussions.
news.summary:
Borosil Renewables plans expansion amid rising solar glass demand.
China's market risks highlighted by Pradeep Kheruka during discussions.

news.title:
Green energy companies showed mixed Q3 FY26 results, with Waaree Energies reporting 119% YoY revenue growth, Suzlon Energy's revenue up 42% YoY, and JSW Energy's profit surging 237% YoY.
KPI Green Energy's revenue grew 45% YoY, Borosil Renewables posted a 61% QoQ profit rise, while Saatvik Green Energy's profit jumped 147% YoY, boosted by strong order books and capacity expansions.
news.summary:
Green energy companies showed mixed Q3 FY26 results, with Waaree Energies reporting 119% YoY revenue growth, Suzlon Energy's revenue up 42% YoY, and JSW Energy's profit surging 237% YoY.
KPI Green Energy's revenue grew 45% YoY, Borosil Renewables posted a 61% QoQ profit rise, while Saatvik Green Energy's profit jumped 147% YoY, boosted by strong order books and capacity expansions.

news.title:
Borosil Renewables targets 30%+ margins supported by steady pricing and renewable power.
Chairman Kheruka notes India’s reliance on imported solar glass sustains demand.
news.summary:
Borosil Renewables targets 30%+ margins supported by steady pricing and renewable power.

news.title:
Borosil Renewables plans 600 TPD capacity expansion by Dec 2026.
The project involves a Rs.950 crore investment.

news.title:
Borosil Renewables reports exceptional Dec 2025 performance with record revenue, margins, and profitability.
Despite operational efficiency gains, share price saw a modest decline amid evolving market conditions.

news.title:

EBITDA margin for Q3 stands at a robust 31.5%.

news.title:
Borosil Renewables' P/S ratio is 4.9x, close to the industry median of 4.3x, reflecting steady revenue growth.
Over three years, revenues grew 112%, aligning with industry momentum, suggesting stable investor sentiment.
news.summary:

news.title:
Borosil Renewables restructures proceeds from preferential equity issue due to undersubscription.
Capital expenditure remains unchanged at Rs. 317.3 Cr; general corporate purposes reduced to Rs. 54.2 Cr.

news.title:
India Ratings affirmed IND A rating for Borosil Renewables, revising outlook to positive.
Improved EBITDA margins, cost savings, and subsidiary deconsolidation drove the outlook revision.
news.summary:

news.title:
